description: Single-session guided meditation journeys with timestamped text prompts. v1.0 ships text-only; voice narration is added in v1.1 by adding voice_clip_id to each prompt.

journeys.name: Body Scan
journeys.description: A 10-minute journey focusing on every part of your body.

prompts.text: Find a comfortable seat. Close your eyes. Take a deep breath. We will begin our body scan journey.
Bring your full attention to the soles of your feet. Notice any tingling or warmth.
Move your focus up to your ankles. Let them go soft and heavy.
Slowly move up to your shins and calves. Let the muscles relax.
Bring awareness to your knees. Notice any tension, and let it dissolve.
Now focus on your thighs. Feel the weight of them against the seat.
Shift your focus to your hips and lower back. Imagine a warm light loosening the muscles there.
Now move to your stomach. Feel it rise and fall with each breath.
Bring awareness to your chest. Feel the expansion of your lungs.
Focus on your hands and fingers. Let them curl naturally. Release all effort.
Move up to your wrists and forearms. Soften them.
Focus on your shoulders. Let them drop away from your ears.
Bring attention to your neck. Move it slightly if it feels tight.
Finally, focus on your face and head. Soften your eyes. Relax your brow.
Feel your whole body as one. Stable. Calm. At peace.
Stay with this feeling of total relaxation. Breathe naturally.
We are now concluding the body scan. When you are ready, gently open your eyes.

journeys.name: Loving-Kindness (Metta)
journeys.description: Cultivate compassion for yourself and others.

prompts.text: Find a comfortable position. We will begin by focusing on ourselves.
Silently repeat: May I be happy. May I be healthy. May I be safe. May I live with ease.
Notice the feeling of warmth in your heart center as you say these words.
Now, think of someone you love. Visualize them clearly. See them smiling.
Direct these same thoughts to them: May you be happy. May you be healthy. May you be safe.
Now, think of a neutral person. Someone you see but don't know well.
Wish them well: May you be happy. May you be safe. May you live with ease.
Now, expand this to all beings everywhere. Without exception.
May all beings be happy. May all beings be free from suffering.
Rest in this feeling of universal compassion as we conclude.

journeys.name: Mantra: So-Hum
journeys.description: A rhythmic meditation using the mantra of being.

prompts.text: We will use the mantra So-Hum. It means 'I am that'.
As you inhale, silently say 'So'. As you exhale, silently say 'Hum'.
Inhale... So. Exhale... Hum.
Continue the rhythm. So... Hum.
If your mind wanders, gently bring it back to the sound. So... Hum.
Breathe in 'So'. Breathe out 'Hum'.
So... Hum. Let the sound fill your mind.
I am that. So... Hum.
So... Hum. Calm and steady.
Rest in the silence of the mantra. So... Hum.
So... Hum.
Gently let go of the mantra. Sit in the quiet for a moment before finishing.
Welcome to this Vipassana session. Settle into your breath.
Observe the breath exactly as it is. Don't try to change it.
Notice the sensation of the air entering and leaving your nostrils.
When a thought arises, simply label it as 'thinking' and return to the breath.
Notice any physical sensations in the body without judgment.
Is there tightness? Warmth? Simply observe its changing nature.
Everything is in flux. Breath, thoughts, sensations. All arising and passing.
Stay present with this flow of experience.
Rest in pure awareness until the bell sounds.
